value.found_quote: The sandbox is the boundary that lets Codex act autonomously without giving it unrestricted access to your machine. When Codex runs local commands in the Codex app, IDE extension, or CLI, those commands run inside a constrained environment instead of running with full access by default.
value.rationale: The page is OpenAI's official Codex documentation describing the sandboxing feature in detail, including how Codex runs commands inside a constrained/sandboxed environment across the app, IDE, and CLI. It describes sandbox modes (read-only, workspace-write, danger-full-access) and platform enforcement, directly substantiating that OpenAI offers a Codex sandbox. The feature is presented as currently available with no indication of deprecation.
